skill.name: frontend-design
skill.description: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, artifacts, posters, or applications (examples include websites, landing pages, dashboards, React components, HTML/CSS layouts, or when styling/beautifying any web UI). Generates creative, polished code and UI design that avoids generic AI aesthetics.
skill.summary: Design polished, distinctive frontend interfaces with production-ready UI guidance.
skill.author: 7Spade

security_audit.summary: The static findings are false positives caused by prose in SKILL.md and standard Apache License text in LICENSE.txt. No executable code, network calls, cryptographic implementation, system reconnaissance, prompt injection, or data exfiltration behavior was found in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: False positive: license URL is documentation only
low_findings.description: LICENSE.txt line 4 contains the Apache License website URL. It is plain license text, not executable code or a network request.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL appears inside the standard Apache License header. There is no code path that opens or transmits data to this URL.

low_findings.title: False positive: weak cryptography patterns are prose
low_findings.description: Static weak cryptography alerts match ordinary words in the Apache License and skill instructions. The files do not contain cryptographic APIs, hashes, ciphers, keys, or implementations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matching lines are Markdown front matter, design guidance, and license prose. No executable cryptographic behavior is present.

low_findings.title: False positive: system reconnaissance terms are design context
low_findings.description: SKILL.md mentions frontend requirements, typography, and background visual details. These are UI design instructions, not operating system inspection commands or data collection behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines only describe interface design choices. I found no commands, scripts, filesystem traversal, environment access, or host reconnaissance logic.

content.value_statement: Generic AI interfaces often look repetitive and weakly connected to user needs. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code toward polished, context-specific frontend design decisions.
actual_capabilities: Guides frontend work toward a clear visual direction before implementation.
Encourages production-grade HTML, CSS, JavaScript, React, or Vue interfaces.
Provides design guidance for typography, color, motion, spacing, and visual details.
Helps avoid common generic AI design patterns and overused visual themes.
Supports pages, components, dashboards, applications, posters, and web artifacts.
limitations: It provides design instructions, not a reusable component library.
It does not include assets, templates, scripts, or automated testing tools.
It depends on the host model and project context for implementation quality.
It does not guarantee accessibility compliance without explicit user requirements.

use_cases.title: Build a memorable landing page
use_cases.description: Create a landing page with a clear concept, strong typography, and cohesive visual treatment.

use_cases.title: Improve an application interface
use_cases.description: Refine an existing dashboard or workflow with better hierarchy, spacing, color, and interaction states.

use_cases.title: Generate polished frontend code
use_cases.description: Ask for working frontend implementation that balances visual originality with production constraints.

prompt_templates.title: Design a simple component
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one focused UI element.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a distinctive pricing card for a developer tool. Use HTML and CSS, with responsive layout and polished interaction states.

prompt_templates.title: Create a full page
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for a complete frontend surface.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a landing page for an independent analytics product. Choose a bold visual direction and implement the full responsive page.

prompt_templates.title: Refine an existing interface
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for improving a current UI.
prompt_templates.prompt: Improve this dashboard screen for clarity and visual quality. Keep the existing data model, but redesign hierarchy, spacing, colors, and controls.

prompt_templates.title: Build a production application view
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request with technical and design constraints.
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a React application view for managing editorial tasks. Include empty, loading, selected, and error states with a distinctive but usable design.

output_examples.input: Design a homepage for a boutique architecture studio.
output_examples.output: A refined page concept with strong type hierarchy, asymmetrical layout, responsive sections, image treatment, and implementation-ready frontend structure.

output_examples.input: Make this SaaS dashboard feel more premium and easier to scan.
output_examples.output: A redesigned dashboard direction with clearer information hierarchy, restrained color use, better spacing, interaction states, and production UI guidance.

output_examples.input: Create a poster-like web artifact for a music event.
output_examples.output: A visually expressive single-page interface with custom typography direction, motion ideas, layered background treatment, and responsive composition.

best_practices: Provide the audience, product goal, content, and technical stack before requesting implementation.
Ask for responsive states, hover states, and empty states when building application interfaces.
Review generated designs for accessibility, performance, and brand fit before publishing.
anti_patterns: Do not request only vague beautification without product context or user goals.
Do not accept decorative effects that reduce readability or task completion.
Do not skip testing on mobile and desktop viewports.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create distinctive frontend components, pages, dashboards, applications, posters, and web artifacts.

faq.question: Does it include executable scripts?
faq.answer: No. The skill contains Markdown guidance and a license file only.

faq.question: Which tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Can it work with React or Vue?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance explicitly allows HTML, CSS, JavaScript, React, Vue, and similar frontend implementation targets.

faq.question: Does it replace a designer?
faq.answer: No. It guides AI-generated frontend work, but human review remains important for brand, accessibility, and usability.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed files contain design guidance and standard license text, with no confirmed security risks.
